title: secretx-bitwarden: unsafe std::env::set_var in tests may need update
description: Scenario: Rust eventually stabilizes std::env::set_var as unsafe (tracking issue exists). The bitwarden tests already use 'unsafe { std::env::set_var(...) }' which suggests future-proofing. But the ENV_LOCK mutex only covers bitwarden's tests; the ambient test runner runs other crates in parallel, and env var mutation across thread boundaries (even in the same process) can cause races if the test binary links multiple crates. Currently the only crate using env var mutation is bitwarden (the others use remove_var-guarded skips, not set_var). Maintenance consequence: if a future crate is added that reads BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN from a different thread (e.g. a shared integration test harness), the lock will not be sufficient. Document the current lock scope and the known limitation explicitly in the test module.
